title: Let the rule of law reign in Asean
primary_topics: liberty and prosperity under the rule of law
sub_topics: Age of Artificial Intelligence and the judiciary
AI-powered Museum for Liberty and Prosperity

stances.claim: ASEAN's adherence to the rule of law — its abhorrence of armed, forcible, insidious, and bullying tactics, including 'gray tactics' — is the foundation of regional peace; ASEAN believes in covenants, treaties, judicial and arbitral tribunals, NOT in the rule of the mob, propaganda, falsehood, or nuclear weapons.
stances.rhetorical_move: negative enumeration (NOT… NOT… NOT…) as rhetorical structure

stances.claim: Truth is eternal and limitless, not bound by sovereignty, territory, ideology, or legality — and the universal truth is that humans need 'justice and jobs; freedom and food; ethics and economics; peace and development; liberty and prosperity.'
stances.rhetorical_move: self-quotation from his 2015 address + universalist framing of the twin-beacons formula

stances.claim: The rule of law is the best method not only for settling disputes peacefully and defending territorial integrity but also for safeguarding liberty and nurturing prosperity.
stances.rhetorical_move: integrative framing — same principle covers external and internal goals

stances.claim: Consensus has always dominated ALA discussions and decisions, forging unity despite diversity in national histories, customs, traditions, religions, economies, sociology, and forms of government.
stances.rhetorical_move: institutional praise + rule-of-unanimity as governance principle

events: ALA General Assembly and Governing Council meetings Kuala Lumpur (October 17-22, 2023)
12th ALA General Assembly (February 26, 2015) — his prior chairmanship assumption

signature_phrases: Let the rule of law reign
justice and jobs; freedom and food; ethics and economics; peace and development; liberty and prosperity
one is useless without the other
NOT in the rule of the mob, NOT in the rule of propaganda and falsehood, NOT in the rule of nuclear weapons
as I fade from my humble leadership post
Before I leave this earth and enter the Great Beyond
decision_framework_signals: applies rule-of-law-vs-rule-of-force binary as the ASEAN regional ethic
frames consensus as ASEAN's operational mechanism for diversity-bridging
uses universalist truth claim ('truth is not bound by sovereignty') to extend liberty-and-prosperity formula globally
cross_references: my address during the 12th ALA General Assembly on Feb. 26, 2015
notable_anecdotes: Gesmundo asking him to continue as ALA Philippines chair because of the Strategic Plan for Judicial Innovation
Negotiating a compromise — 'an ancient relic' staying until end of 2023, with Gesmundo taking over 2024
